entries.excerpt: I’ve confirmed the root cause from task-owned evidence: the default pawn is still hardwired to the old `ZM_ABP_Unarmed` lane that Task-0006 previously proved unsafe. I’m promoting the repaired aligned anim class into `ThirdPersonPawn` now and adding the same runtime mesh-state logging there so the default-lane PIE evidence is auditable in `ThirdPerson.log`.

Concrete findings to use: the default-lane bug reproduces in Saved/GameAutomation/20260419T144717Z-9DF534D8415355F76DD9D3B25C6C6551-task0006-default-lane-diagnosis. lane_c_viewport.png and lane_c_pawn_proof.png both show the invisible pawn. lane-c-presentation.txt shows the default pawn is still using the legacy broken assets: mesh=/Game/Experimental/ZeroMale/Character/SK_ZeroMale_FirstPass and anim=/Game/Experimental/ZeroMale/Animations/ZM_ABP_Unarmed.ZM_ABP_Unarmed_C. That legacy lane was already failing validator smoke for local translation plausibility in Tracking/Task-0006/Testing/validator-smoke-existing-zeromale-report.json. The repaired assets live on the automation-pilot lane: mesh=/Game/Experimental/ZeroMaleAutomationPilot/Character/SK_ZeroMale_AutomationPilot and aligned anim=/Game/ZMAALN_ABP_Unarmed.ZMAALN_ABP_Unarmed_C. Also, local engine API exposes editor PIE automation through ULevelEditorSubsystem::EditorRequestBeginPlay / EditorRequestEndPlay in Engine/Source/Editor/LevelEditor/Public/LevelEditorSubsystem.h. Use this to keep final proof on the human default lane if needed. Proceed with durable state update, open/update the task-owned bug for the default-lane invisible pawn, implement the fix, and verify on the default lane.

Using the concrete default-lane evidence you surfaced. I already reopened the task and recorded the bug; now I’m wiring `ThirdPersonPawn` off the legacy broken asset pair and onto the validated automation-pilot mesh plus aligned anim lane, then I’ll rebuild and verify on the repo-default path.

entries.excerpt:
Continue Task-0006 PASS-0009 now.

Human-approved runtime-only remaining defects to document and fix:
1. On the runtime default lane, the feet read spherical-like / blob-like.
2. On the runtime default lane, the feet look offset relative to the ground, and the run presents a fast side-to-side rocking / tilt read in the animated mesh or pose.

Requirements:
- Update durable artifacts immediately so these two runtime-only defects are stated clearly as the remaining problem. Do not dilute them with non-runtime evidence.
- Keep the goal post pinned to the human default runtime lane only. Non-game/source/export renders may support debugging, but they must not be used to dismiss the runtime defect.
- Iterate on a fix repo-locally. No engine modifications. Do not touch REGRESSION.md.
- Preserve prior pass history honestly; this is PASS-0009 work.
- Rerun unattended default-lane proof after changes.
- Close only if the runtime lane itself looks and measures fixed; otherwise record the remaining runtime defect honestly.
- Commit and push meaningful checkpoints.

Deliverables:
- updated BUG-0004 / HANDOFF / TASK-STATE with the two agreed runtime defects
- code changes for the fix
- fresh runtime-only proof artifacts
- honest conclusion on whether the spherical-read and offset/rocking defects are actually fixed

I’m updating the task artifacts first so `PASS-0009` explicitly tracks the two remaining runtime-only defects on the human default lane before I touch code: blob-like feet read, and offset/rocking during live run.

excerpts.reason_kept: Shows the human reasserting ownership expectations and the agent re-committing to a concrete, non-tweak debugging seam.

entries.excerpt: Continue PASS-0009 now under your existing ownership. Do not stop after a failed incremental attempt and do not hand intermediate failure back to the user. Stay inside the runtime human default lane only; do not use source/export/non-runtime images to dismiss runtime defects. The active defects to keep grounded are: (1) feet read round/blob-like at runtime, (2) feet look offset and the run reads with side-to-side rocking/tilt in the animated mesh/pose, and (3) both feet hover above the ground. Your last pass proved MaxSpeed=500 and mesh Z=-106 helped but did not fix it. Narrow the remaining cause beyond that retune, likely around the default pawn's single-node animation / pose-state grounding seam, implement the next justified repo-local fix, rerun default-lane proof, and keep durable state current at each checkpoint. Commit and push meaningful checkpoints. Only stop if you reach real closure or a real external block.

I’m staying in PASS-0009 and pushing past the last failed increment. The next step is to inspect the default pawn’s live single-node animation contract itself, because the `500` speed and `-106` mount proved the global retune was only part of the problem.

I’ve got one strong clue from the live artifacts: the foot height is not just globally wrong, it changes a lot by animation state. That points away from “just lower the mesh more” and toward the single-node state contract itself.

The current task-owned checker is also part of the problem: it still lets a lane “pass” when the human-facing lower-body read is plainly wrong. I’m inspecting the runtime driver and the existing `GroundSpeed`/`ShouldMove` seams together so I can either repair the single-node lane or switch to a more truthful runtime contract without guessing.

excerpts.reason_kept: Shows the explicit root-cause debugging contract: define the first concrete disagreement with values, then walk upstream through writers.

entries.excerpt: Tighten PASS-0009 to the shared debugging method from C:\Users\gregs\.codex\Orchestration\Processes\DEBUGGING.md. The task is not 'improve the look' and not 'another bounded tweak.' It is: identify the first concrete disagreement in runtime default-lane state, then trace that exact bad state upstream through the code and data that write or advance it until root cause is proven. Do not stop at 'single-node seam' as a category. Name the concrete disagreement with values. Examples of acceptable disagreement seams: left/right foot world Z or toe world Z vs ground/capsule bottom at a frame where the human says both feet are hovering; unexpected pelvis/mesh/root roll or lateral offset at a frame where the run reads like side-to-side rocking; unexpected component-space foot transforms while the capsule remains upright. Then trace writers/updaters upstream one boundary at a time: runtime bone/component/world transforms -> mesh relative transform / component transform -> animation asset/time/playback state or runtime pose evaluation -> whichever exact writer makes the state wrong. Update BUG-0004 and REGRESSION-RUN artifacts around that narrowing path. Keep all evidence on the human default lane. Do not use source/export renders to dismiss runtime defects. Continue until you can state confirmed cause or a tightly bounded remaining branch with contradictory evidence preserved.

I’m tightening `PASS-0009` to the shared debugging method now. First step is to re-ground on the shared debugging contract, confirm there isn’t a stray editor process from the aborted turn, and then define the first concrete runtime-state disagreement with values before touching the diagnosis again.
